palette: Soviet brutalist concrete (cool grey), opera house gold (warm yellow), mountain twilight (deep indigo), and flickering neon (magenta).
lighting: Blue hour fading into night. Harsh fluorescent interior light clashing with soft golden streetlamps and the cold glow of the Tian Shan peaks in the distance.
instagram_prompts: First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture from a low angle on a concrete ledge. Looking out over the Bishkek Opera and Ballet Theatre at dusk. The building is lit with warm gold spotlights against a deep indigo sky. The surrounding brutalist apartment blocks are silhouetted in cool greys. No people, no vehicles. Impossibly large world. No overlays.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture from the corner of a dusty desk in a hotel room. A single old philosophical paperback (Title: 'Problems of Philosophy') rests next to a half-drunk cup of dark Kyrgyz tea. The room is dim, lit only by a flickering neon magenta sign from the street reflecting on the window glass. Microscale POV, world feels enormous. No overlays.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture at ground level on a cracked pavement in Bishkek. A discarded festival flyer (Jazz Festival 2026) is caught in a tuft of dry grass growing between the concrete slabs. In the background, the blurry lights of a Soviet-era monument. Cold, high-altitude light. World is impossibly large. No Lil Mike. No overlays.
